sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The tools.pyx module is a vital utility component within the AnalysisG framework, offering a suite of high-performance functions implemented in Cython. These functions are designed to bridge the gap between Python and C++ data types, facilitate efficient string manipulation, and provide convenient file system operations. This module is extensively used across the framework to ensure seamless interaction between Python-level logic and underlying C++ functionalities.

sect1.title: Helper Functions (C-style)
sect1.para: These functions are implemented as C-style functions in Cython for maximum performance and direct interaction with C++ types.

listitem.para:
cdef string enc(str val): Encodes a Python str object into a C++ std::string (UTF-8 encoding).
val
The Python string to encode.
A C++ std::string.

cdef str env(string val): Decodes a C++ std::string object into a Python str (UTF-8 decoding).
val
The C++ std::string to decode.
A Python str.

cdef list env_vec(vector[string]* inpt): Converts a C++ std::vector<std::string> (passed as a pointer) into a Python list of str objects.
inpt
A pointer to a C++ std::vector<std::string>.
A Python list of str.

cdef vector[string] enc_list(list inpt): Converts a Python list of str objects into a C++ std::vector<std::string>.
inpt
A Python list of str.
A C++ std::vector<std::string>.

cdef dict as_basic_dist_dict_f(map[string, map[float, base_types]]* inpt): Converts a nested C++ std::map<std::string, std::map<float, base_types>> to a Python dictionary.
inpt
A pointer to the C++ map.
A Python dict.

cdef list as_list(vector[base_types]* inp): Converts a C++ std::vector<base_types> (passed as a pointer) into a Python list.
inp
A pointer to the C++ std::vector<base_types>.
A Python list.

cdef dict as_basic_dict(map[string, base_types]* inpt): Converts a C++ std::map<std::string, base_types> (passed as a pointer) into a Python dict.
inpt
A pointer to the C++ std::map.
A Python dict.

cdef dict as_basic_udict(unordered_map[string, base_types]* inpt): Converts a C++ std::unordered_map<std::string, base_types> (passed as a pointer) into a Python dict.
inpt
A pointer to the C++ std::unordered_map.
A Python dict.

cdef dict as_basic_dict_dict(map[string, map[string, base_types]]* inpt): Converts a nested C++ std::map<std::string, std::map<std::string, base_types>> (passed as a pointer) into a Python dict.
inpt
A pointer to the nested C++ std::map.
A Python dict.

cdef dict as_dict(map[string, vector[base_types]]* inpt): Converts a C++ std::map<std::string, std::vector<base_types>> (passed as a pointer) into a Python dict.
inpt
A pointer to the C++ std::map.
A Python dict.

cdef dict as_dict_dict(map[string, map[string, vector[base_types]]]* inpt): Converts a nested C++ std::map<std::string, std::map<std::string, std::vector<base_types>>> (passed as a pointer) into a Python dict.
inpt
A pointer to the nested C++ std::map.
A Python dict.

cdef dict as_dict_dict_dict(map[string, map[string, map[string, vector[base_types]]]]* inpt): Converts a triple-nested C++ std::map<std::string, std::map<std::string, std::map<std::string, std::vector<base_types>>>> (passed as a pointer) into a Python dict.
inpt
A pointer to the triple-nested C++ std::map.
A Python dict.

cdef void as_map(dict inpt, map[string, base_types]* out): Converts a Python dict into a C++ std::map<std::string, base_types> (passed as a pointer out). Handles conversion of string values to C++ std::string.
inpt
The Python dict to convert.
out
A pointer to the C++ std::map to populate.

cdef void as_umap(dict inpt, unordered_map[string, base_types]* out): Converts a Python dict into a C++ std::unordered_map<std::string, base_types> (passed as a pointer out). Handles conversion of string values to C++ std::string.
inpt
The Python dict to convert.
out
A pointer to the C++ std::unordered_map to populate.

sect1.title: The Tools Class
sect1.para: Tools is a cdef class that acts as a Python wrapper for a collection of C++ utility functions, providing convenient access to file system operations, string manipulations, and data processing utilities.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ tools object and assigns its pointer to self.ptr.
__init__(self): The Python initializer, intentionally left empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ tools object pointed to by self.ptr.

create_path(self, str pth): Creates a directory path, including any necessary parent directories.
pth
The path to create.

delete_path(self, str pth): Deletes a file or an empty directory.
pth
The path to delete.

is_file(self, str pth): Checks if a given path corresponds to an existing file.
pth
The path to check.
True if it's a file, False otherwise.

rename(self, str src, str dst): Renames or moves a file or directory from src to dst.
src
The source path.
dst
The destination path.

abs(self, str pth): Returns the absolute path for a given path.
pth
The path to resolve.
The absolute path as a Python string.

ls(self, str pth, str ext): Lists files in a directory matching a given extension.
pth
The directory path.
ext
The file extension to filter by (e.g., ".root").
A Python list of matching file paths.

replace(self, str val, str rpl, str rpwl): Replaces occurrences of a substring rpl with rpwl within the string val.
val
The original string.
rpl
The substring to replace.
rpwl
The replacement substring.
The modified string.

has_substring(self, str val, str rpl): Checks if a string val contains a substring rpl.
val
The string to search within.
rpl
The substring to search for.
True if rpl is found, False otherwise.

ends_with(self, str val, str rpl): Checks if a string val ends with a substring rpl.
val
The string to check.
rpl
The suffix to check for.
True if val ends with rpl, False otherwise.

has_value(self, list data, str trg): Checks if a list of strings data contains a target string trg.
data
A Python list of strings.
trg
The target string to search for.
True if trg is found in data, False otherwise.

split(self, str data, trg): Splits a string data by a delimiter trg (which can be a string or an integer for splitting at a specific index).
data
The string to split.
trg
The delimiter (string) or index (int) to split by.
A Python list of substrings.

hash(self, str data, int lx = 8): Computes a hash for the given string data.
data
The string to hash.
lx
The length of the hash string to return (default 8).
The hash as a Python string.

encode64(self, str data): Base64 encodes a string.
data
The string to encode.
The base64 encoded string.

decode64(self, str data): Base64 decodes a string.
data
The string to decode.
The base64 decoded string.

discretize(self, list data, int lx): Discretizes a list of data (strings or integers).
data
A Python list of strings or integers.
lx
The discretization parameter.
A discretized list.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The tools.pyx module extensively uses Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class Tools: Declares Tools as a Cython extension type.
cdef tools *ptr: A C++ pointer to a tools object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp cimport bool, int, map, pair, string, vector: Imports C++ standard library types.
from libcpp.unordered_map cimport unordered_map: Imports C++ std::unordered_map.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports the C++ tools class declaration and base_types from its .pxd file.
cython.operator.dereference as dref: Used to dereference C++ pointers.
